description: This is your strongest domain already — you're serving dynamo via a Django streaming SSE API. So this roadmap skips 'how Django works' entirely and goes straight to production patterns: advanced Django internals, DRF depth, async backends (FastAPI), caching, background jobs, WebSockets, and auth — aimed at scalable APIs, AI inference backends, and real auth systems.

topics.title: Advanced Django (signals, middleware, scaling)
topics.note: Assumes Django basics + your existing SSE streaming view are already solid.

subtopics.title: Signals: when they help (decoupled side effects) vs when they become debugging nightmares
Custom middleware: request/response processing, timing, auth injection
ORM performance: select_related/prefetch_related, query optimization, N+1 detection with django-debug-toolbar
Database connection pooling and scaling reads (read replicas) for a high-traffic inference API
ASGI in Django: moving your SSE streaming view onto Django Channels/ASGI properly for real concurrency
Horizontal scaling patterns: stateless Django workers behind a load balancer, session storage in Redis instead of local

topics.title: Django REST Framework — Advanced
topics.note: You've started DRF — this pushes into the parts that matter for real APIs.

subtopics.title: Custom serializers: nested writes, validation logic, SerializerMethodField for computed fields
ViewSets, routers, and custom actions (@action decorator) for non-CRUD endpoints (like inference triggers)
Permissions & throttling: custom permission classes, per-user rate limiting for an inference API
API versioning strategies and pagination for large result sets
Testing DRF endpoints properly: APITestCase, factory-based fixtures, mocking model inference calls

topics.title: Async Backend (FastAPI)
topics.note: New framework, but highest leverage for AI inference backends since it's async-native and what most production ML serving uses.

subtopics.title: FastAPI fundamentals: async def routes, Pydantic models, dependency injection
Async streaming responses (StreamingResponse) — rebuild your dynamo SSE endpoint in FastAPI and compare to Django's version
async/await deep dive: event loop, asyncio.gather, avoiding blocking calls (critical since PyTorch inference is often sync/blocking)
Serving PyTorch models in FastAPI: batching requests, GPU queueing, avoiding OOM under concurrent load
resources: BentoML/TorchServe docs for patterns, even if you self-roll it

subtopics.title: WebSocket support in FastAPI for bidirectional agent communication
Background tasks in FastAPI (BackgroundTasks) vs when you actually need Celery
Redis fundamentals: data types (strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets), TTLs
Django/DRF caching with Redis: per-view cache, cache invalidation strategies
Caching model outputs / KV-cache-style patterns for repeated inference requests
resources: Self-directed: cache dynamo responses for identical prompts

subtopics.title: Redis as a message broker for Celery and pub/sub for real-time features
Redis persistence & eviction policies (RDB vs AOF, LRU eviction) — production ops awareness
Celery fundamentals: tasks, workers, brokers (Redis as broker)
Task retries, idempotency, and dead-letter handling for failed jobs
Long-running tasks: training job orchestration (kick off a Kaggle-style training run as a Celery task with progress tracking)
resources: Self-directed: wrap dynamo training loop trigger in Celery

subtopics.title: Periodic tasks with Celery Beat (scheduled retraining, cleanup jobs)
Monitoring Celery in production: Flower, task result backends

topics.title: WebSockets (real-time apps)
topics.note: You've done SSE (one-way); this adds true bidirectional real-time, needed for agent control panels.

subtopics.title: WebSocket protocol fundamentals and where it beats SSE (bidirectional, lower overhead for chat)
Django Channels: consumers, routing, channel layers backed by Redis
Scaling WebSocket connections: connection limits, sticky sessions, horizontal scaling with Redis channel layer
Build a bidirectional agent control channel: send interrupt/approval signals to a running agent, receive live tool-call events
resources: Self-directed, ties into your Groq multi-agent tool

subtopics.title: JWT fundamentals: structure, signing (HS256 vs RS256), expiry, refresh token rotation
Implementing JWT auth in DRF (djangorestframework-simplejwt) and in FastAPI (python-jose/fastapi-users)
OAuth2 flows: authorization code flow, PKCE for SPAs, and implementing 'Sign in with GitHub' (fits your GitHub-heavy workflow)
Session security: secure cookie flags, CSRF protection, token storage tradeoffs (localStorage vs httpOnly cookies)
Role-based access control (RBAC) for multi-user agent dashboards (admin vs viewer permissions on agent runs)

topics.title: Goal Projects: Scalable APIs, AI inference backends, Auth systems

subtopics.title: Migrate/duplicate dynamo's inference API to FastAPI with async streaming + request batching
Add Redis caching + Celery-based async training triggers to the dynamo backend
Build full JWT + OAuth (GitHub login) auth system in front of the dynamo API for multi-user access
Build the backend half of the agent control panel: WebSocket channel streaming live tool calls from your Groq multi-agent tool
resources: Self-directed project, pairs with frontend capstone
